name: Context injection
subtitle: Reads project-docs/ into .synlynk/context.md before each exec
description: Before every `synlynk exec` call, the harness reads `project-docs/memory.md`, `roadmap.md`, and `todo.md` into a single `.synlynk/context.md` file that is prepended to the agent's prompt. This ensures every AI session starts with current project state — no repeated setup, no lost context.

usage.note: Generates .synlynk/context.md then spawns claude with context prepended

name: Flatline detection
subtitle: Alerts and halts when the last 3 runs share identical command + non-zero exit
description: synlynk tracks the last 100 exec invocations in `.synlynk/telemetry.json`. If the most recent 3 entries share the same command and all exited non-zero, the harness prints a flatline warning and blocks the next run. This prevents silent looping where an agent repeatedly fails at the same step without human review.

usage.note: After 3 consecutive failures: FLATLINE ALERT — same command, 3 non-zero exits

name: Cost tracking
subtitle: Appends per-session token counts and $ cost to project-docs/costs.md
description: After each `synlynk exec` run, the harness scrapes token counts from captured AI CLI stdout using format-specific regex patterns, computes cost at hardcoded rates ($0.003/1K input + $0.015/1K output), appends a row to `project-docs/costs.md`, and prints a Budget Pulse summary to the terminal.

usage.note: Prints: Budget Pulse | session: $0.24 | total: $3.12 | 14 runs

name: Budget enforcement
subtitle: Blocks exec when cumulative cost or request count exceeds configured limits
description: Before spawning any AI subprocess, `check_budgets()` reads `.synlynk/config.json` for `limit_usd` and `limit_requests`, then compares against totals in `.synlynk/telemetry.json`. If either limit is exceeded, the exec is blocked with a clear message. Limits are project-specific and set at `synlynk init` time.

usage.note: If over budget: Budget limit exceeded ($50.00). Run synlynk reset to continue.

name: Telemetry log
subtitle: Rolling 100-entry log of every exec — duration, exit code, cost, command
description: Every `synlynk exec` invocation appends a structured JSON record to `.synlynk/telemetry.json`, keeping only the last 100 entries. Each record includes: timestamp, command, duration_seconds, exit_code, input_tokens, output_tokens, and estimated_cost_usd. This log powers both flatline detection and budget enforcement.

usage.note: Inspect last 100 exec records

name: gh CLI identity
subtitle: Resolves verified GitHub login as the canonical session actor for devlog attribution
description: When attributing devlog entries, `get_username()` calls `gh api user --jq .login` first, falling back to `git config user.name` only if gh is unavailable or unauthenticated. The GitHub login is a verified identity tied to a real account, unlike git config which can be set to any value. This is the v0.2.x approximation of the full Ed25519 identity stack planned for v0.5.0.

usage.note: Devlog entry attributed to authenticated GitHub user (e.g. nikhilsoman)

name: Polyglot agent files
subtitle: Generates CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md (AGY), and AGENTS.md from a single parameterised template
description: `synlynk init` generates three agent instruction files — `CLAUDE.md` (Claude Code), `GEMINI.md` (AntiGravity/AGY CLI), and `AGENTS.md` (Codex/OpenAI CLI) — from `_build_templates(org, repo, project_id)`. All three files contain the same session protocol, live issues SOP, git worktree policy, and GitHub Projects v2 GraphQL block. This ensures every agent in the workgroup receives identical constraints from a single source of truth. As of v0.4.1, Gemini CLI is replaced by AGY (AntiGravity) as the third trio member.

usage.note: Generates CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md (AGY), AGENTS.md with live GraphQL blocks

changelog.note: Gemini CLI replaced by AGY (AntiGravity); GEMINI.md template updated to agy-2.x, no transition note

name: Parametric init
subtitle: Four --flags fill project-specific values into all generated files at init time
description: `synlynk init` accepts `--agents`, `--mode`, `--org`, `--repo`, and `--project-id` flags. The `--project-id` flag is the most consequential: it fills the GitHub Projects v2 node ID (`PJ_kwDO…`) into live GraphQL mutation blocks in all agent instruction files, making them immediately executable rather than containing placeholder text.

usage.cmd: synlynk init --mode team --org acme --repo api --project-id PJ_kwDOA1234
usage.note: All agent files generated with org, repo, and project ID filled in

name: Agent discovery
subtitle: init wizard scans for Claude, Gemini, Codex, and AGY — shows your workgroup at a glance
description: `synlynk init` now runs a 6-step wizard. Step 2 is Magic Moment 1: it probes each known agent CLI with `--version`, then prints a workgroup table showing every installed agent with its roles (Architect / Builder / Verifier) and a functional indicator. `discover_agents()` reads `AGENT_CAPABILITY_BASELINES` for role and flag definitions, checks `~/.claude`, `~/.gemini`, etc. for config directories, and supports per-project path overrides in `.synlynk/config.json`. An opt-in LLM enrichment step calls the best available agent non-interactively to synthesise an informed `roadmap.md` from git history and the README.

usage.note: Wizard discovers agents, shows workgroup table, bootstraps project-docs/, offers LLM enrichment

name: Background dispatch
subtitle: Fire-and-forget agent jobs from any shell — synlynk dispatch claude --task "..."
description: `synlynk dispatch <agent> --task <text>` launches the agent CLI in the background using `subprocess.Popen(start_new_session=True)`, capturing stdout to `.synlynk/logs/<job_id>.log` and persisting the PID to `.synlynk/jobs.json`. On every startup, `_reconcile_jobs()` probes live PIDs and marks dead ones as completed or failed. `synlynk jobs` lists running/recent jobs; `synlynk logs --job <id>` tails output in real time.

usage.note: Launches claude in background, returns job ID immediately
Lists all running and recent jobs with status, agent, and task
Tails the last 50 lines of a job's stdout log

name: Parallel trio dispatch
subtitle: synlynk run --trio dispatches one task to all functional agents simultaneously
description: `synlynk run --trio <task>` dispatches the same task description to every functional agent (claude, agy, codex — whichever are installed) in parallel background jobs. Each agent runs independently; `synlynk jobs` shows all three. This is the first form of horizontal parallelism in synlynk: same task, multiple perspectives, results streamed to separate log files for human review.

usage.cmd: synlynk run --trio "write unit tests for src/auth.py"
usage.note: Dispatches to all functional agents in parallel; each gets its own job ID

name: Instruction reach
subtitle: Extends synlynk context injection to Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Windsurf
description: v0.4.1 extends `synlynk init` from 3 CLI targets to 7 tracked instruction files. In addition to `CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`, and `AGENTS.md`, synlynk now writes tool-native instruction blocks into `.cursor/rules/synlynk.mdc` (Cursor, with `alwaysApply: true` MDC frontmatter), `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (GitHub Copilot), `.windsurfrules` (Windsurf), and `AI_INSTRUCTIONS.md` (universal fallback). Three marker styles are used to preserve user content: `html` comments, `hash` (`# synlynk:start`), and `none` (synlynk owns the whole file). Cursor and Copilot targets are only written if their respective config directories exist.

usage.note: Writes synlynk section to all 7 instruction targets; creates .cursor/rules/synlynk.mdc if .cursor/ exists
Shows status (ok / missing / drifted / untracked / no-markers) for each tracked file

name: Instruction drift detection
subtitle: Fires INSTRUCTION_DRIFT sentinel when a tracked instruction file is edited outside synlynk
description: Every `synlynk exec` call runs `_check_instruction_drift()`, which loads `.synlynk/instructions.json` (the SHA manifest written by `init`) and compares the SHA of each file's synlynk-owned section against the stored value. If any file has been edited, an `INSTRUCTION_DRIFT` alert is written to `sentinel.md`. The sentinel fires once per change — not once per exec — by updating the manifest SHA immediately after firing. Use `synlynk instructions update <file>` to regenerate the section, or `synlynk instructions ack <file>` to dismiss the alert without regenerating.

usage.note: Shows user content outside synlynk markers in each tracked file
Regenerates the synlynk section and resets its SHA in the manifest
Removes INSTRUCTION_DRIFT lines from sentinel.md without regenerating

name: Capability engine
subtitle: Routes tasks to the best agent based on a live SQLite ledger of quality scores
description: v0.5.0 introduces data-driven agent routing. `_best_agent_for_story(story_id)` queries the `capability_scores` SQLite view, which aggregates human ratings, verifier pass rates, and auto-quality signals across a 3D domain taxonomy (engineering domain × org domain × industry). A cold-start round-robin runs for the first 3 samples per (agent, domain) triple; recency-weighted scoring takes over after that. The `synlynk story create/list` and `synlynk score add/list` commands provide the CLI surface for the capability ledger.

usage.cmd: synlynk story create "Implement OAuth flow" --domain auth
usage.note: Creates a story record in state.db, returns a story_id
Records a human quality rating (1–5) for a completed run
Shows aggregated capability scores per agent per domain

name: Model version probing
subtitle: Records which model version ran each job — enabling split-model detection and honest routing
description: v0.6.0 adds four-tier model version resolution. At dispatch time, `_probe_model_version()` fires a `--version`-style call to the agent CLI and scrapes the model identifier (e.g. `claude-opus-4-8`). This becomes `model_at_dispatch` on the job record. A `# synlynk-meta` block in the agent's output can supply `model_at_completion` (Tier 1, highest trust). If both are known and differ, `split_model=1` is set, excluding the run from capability aggregation. `synlynk score attest` allows retroactive attestation when the agent reports its model post-run.

usage.note: Retroactively records which model completed the job
Blocks merge if any job in the PR used an unknown or mismatched model version

name: Source architecture injection
subtitle: ## Source Architecture snapshot injected into every exec context from a passive git-HEAD cache
description: v0.7.0 adds a language-agnostic static scanner that extracts symbols from up to 9 languages (Python, JS, TS, Go, Rust, Ruby, Java, Kotlin, Shell) and injects a `## Source Architecture` section into every `synlynk exec` context. The scan cache is keyed by git HEAD SHA — zero overhead on cache hit. A file scoring formula (entry-point bonus, git frequency, depth penalty) surfaces the 15 most relevant files. Full results are stored in the `source_symbols` SQLite table and `project-docs/source-map.md`. `synlynk scan [--deep] [--status]` provides manual control.

usage.note: Scans source tree and injects ## Source Architecture into context; zero cost on cache hit
Full scan: writes source_symbols to state.db and source-map.md
Shows cache age, git HEAD SHA, and file/symbol counts

name: Scoped dispatch context
subtitle: Task-level context slice replaces 7-day devlog dump in every agent dispatch
description: v0.9.0 introduces `generate_context(scope="task:<story_id>")`. Instead of injecting the full project context (7-day devlog, all teammate activity, complete memory file) into every dispatch, `_generate_task_context()` builds a minimal snapshot: story metadata from state.db, active tasks only from todo.md, and up to 20 source files filtered by the story's engineering domain from the scan cache. This reduces token overhead per dispatch by 60–80% and keeps agents focused on the relevant subsystem.

usage.cmd: synlynk dispatch claude --story story-abc123 --task "add OAuth flow"
usage.note: Agent receives story metadata + active tasks + domain-filtered source files only

name: Dispatch quality enhancements
subtitle: ## Relevant Files, ## How to Verify, and per-agent prompt framing in every dispatch
description: v0.9.0 adds three quality layers to every agent dispatch. `_relevant_files_for_story()` injects a `## Relevant Files` section (up to 10 paths from the source-map, filtered by engineering domain) so agents know exactly which files to touch. `_verify_contract_for_story()` injects a `## How to Verify` section with a concrete pytest invocation derived from the story title. `_format_prompt_for_agent()` applies agent-specific framing: Codex receives `## Task Criteria` as scannable bullets; AGY receives a concise `Task:` prefix with 2000-char context; Claude receives the full narrative prompt.

usage.cmd: synlynk dispatch codex --story story-abc123 --task "fix token extraction"
usage.note: Codex receives Task Criteria bullets + Relevant Files list + How to Verify pytest command

name: Ed25519 capability signing
subtitle: Every capability rating is signed with a local Ed25519 key — tamper-evident from day one
description: v0.9.0 wires the `ed25519_sig` column in the `capability_ratings` table that has existed since v0.5.0. `_ensure_identity_key()` creates `~/.synlynk/identity.key` via `ssh-keygen -t ed25519` on first use. `_sign_capability_rating()` signs a canonical `json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True)` payload of the rating fields via `ssh-keygen -Y sign`, then stores the signature alongside the rating. This is the first step toward the Tokq-compatible signed capability ledger planned for v1.0.0.

usage.note: Creates ~/.synlynk/identity.key and prints the public key
Rating INSERT now includes ed25519_sig field populated automatically

name: Async daemon
subtitle: Always-on process — agents pull context over HTTP without invoking the CLI
description: `synlynk daemon start` double-forks a background process that keeps `context.md` current via mtime polling and exposes it over a local HTTP API on `localhost:27471`. A persistent `daemon_jobs` queue in `state.db` tracks jobs with priority ordering, dependency chains, and crash-safe per-job commits. `--install-service` registers the daemon with launchd (macOS), systemd user units (Linux), or a `@reboot` crontab entry as a fallback — so it survives reboots without manual intervention.

usage.note: Fork-daemonize; writes .synlynk/daemon.pid; HTTP server starts on localhost:27471
Shows pid, uptime, queue depth (queued/running/done/failed), and HTTP URL
Registers with launchd (macOS) / systemd (Linux) / crontab (fallback) for auto-start on login
Agents fetch current context.md without invoking synlynk exec
Enqueue a job; daemon picks it up on the next poll tick
